text: This is the assembly process of this reactor. Step 1, this is the cathode flow plate. It
has a serpentine flow field. Step 2, this is the cathode electrode. The cathode is prepared
by spray coating copper oxide catalysis onto H2C3 carbon paper. The catalysis loading is
3 micrometer per centimeter square. Step 3, this is the cathode gasket. The gasket thickness
is 0.2 micrometer and the indoor window size is 1 centimeter by 1 centimeter.
Step 4, we will place an alkaline ion exchange membrane. This membrane is alkaline ion exchange
membrane. The membrane is cysteine X3760 bioform dioxide materials. Step 5, this is
the middle chamber. The chamber thickness is 10 micrometer and the window size is also
1 by 1 centimeter. The chamber is used to hold the solid electrolyte. This blue one
is solid electrolyte. The solid electrolyte is strong acid cation exchange resin beads
with a particle size of 600 to 800 micrometer. The resin beads are filled to fully occupy
the middle chamber.
Step 6, we place this gasket. This is the anode gasket. The anode gasket thickness is
also 0.2 micrometer and the active window for this gasket is also 1 by 1 centimeter.
Step 7, we place the anode side membrane.
This is the anode side membrane. This is a cation exchange membrane or CAM. The membrane
is Leven 117. Step 8, this is the anode. Number 8, this is the anode electrode. The anode
electrode is prepared by spray coating PT-Rosilium catalysis onto H23C3 carbon paper. The catalyst
loading is 2 milligram centimeter square and the actual area is also 1 by 1. Step 9, this
is the anode flow plate. It also has a flow field. Finally, after aligning all components,
we will assemble and tighten the electrode using 0.3 newton meter.
We will feed the cathode with CO gas and we will feed the anode with 99 wattage of
hydrogen plus 1 percentage CO.
